name: Abdullah ibn Abbas
title: Scholar of Bani Hashem
shortDescription: The cousin of the Prophet (PBUH) and great Quranic exegete who tried extensively with his arguments to dissuade the Imam from going to Kufa.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Abbas, the cousin of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Amir al-Mu'minin (AS), was one of the greatest scholars, exegetes, and politicians of the Bani Hashem.

When Imam Hussain (AS) intended to depart for Kufa from Mecca, Ibn Abbas, aware of the Kufans' disloyalty during the times of Imam Ali (AS) and Imam Hasan (AS), became extremely worried. He went to see the Imam several times, insistently asking him not to go to Kufa and instead travel to Yemen, which was a Shia base.

Imam Hussain (AS) told him: 'O cousin! I know you speak out of compassion and kindness, but I have made my decision.' When Ibn Abbas saw he couldn't dissuade the Imam, he said: 'Then at least do not take the women and children with you.'

Ibn Abbas, who couldn't join the caravan due to his blindness, stayed in Mecca. When the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom reached Mecca, Ibn Abbas wept bitterly and mourned the Master of Martyrs until the end of his life.
quoteArabic: يا ابْنَ عَمِّ، إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ ناصِحٌ مُشْفِقٌ، وَلَكِنِّي قَدْ أَزْمَعْتُ المَسِيرَ...
quote: (The Imam said to him:) O cousin! I know you are a compassionate well-wisher, but I have resolved to go...
